# Sieve

> Sieve is a transparent proxy that sits between an agent framework and an
> LLM endpoint (OpenAI-compatible or Ollama). It strips tool schemas,
> repeated instructions, and stale history from every outbound turn,
> rewrites the prompt into a lean payload, and backs retrieval with an
> encrypted local memory store. The agent and the endpoint are unchanged;
> the proxy does the reduction. Zero telemetry, local-first, Apache 2.0.

Key facts: install with `pipx install llm-sieve`, then `sieve-install`
for a guided setup. The proxy exposes `/api/chat` (Ollama style) and
`/v1/chat/completions` (OpenAI style). User data lives in an encrypted
SQLite store under `~/.sieve/` and survives package upgrades.

## Docs

- [Getting started](https://llmsieve.dev/getting-started/): blank terminal to working proxy in about a minute
- [Installation](https://llmsieve.dev/installation/): pipx, pip, and from-source paths; platform notes; upgrades
- [Configuration](https://llmsieve.dev/configuration/): the single YAML config file and every key it accepts
- [CLI reference](https://llmsieve.dev/cli-reference/): every command, flag, and option
- [Diagnostic headers](https://llmsieve.dev/diagnostic-headers/): response headers for operational visibility
- [Changelog](https://llmsieve.dev/changelog/): release history (Keep a Changelog, SemVer)

## Blog

- [Why Sieve](https://llmsieve.dev/blog/2026/06/09/why-sieve/): proxy vs library vs compaction — the architectural trade-off
- [The hidden cost of context](https://llmsieve.dev/blog/2026/06/10/the-hidden-cost-of-context/): why agent token bills grow faster than expected
- [Compute is the bottleneck](https://llmsieve.dev/blog/2026/06/09/compute-is-the-bottleneck-tokens-are-just-the-price-tag/): the compute economics behind token pricing

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/llmsieve/llm-sieve): Apache 2.0, issues and contributions welcome
- [PyPI package](https://pypi.org/project/llm-sieve/): `pip install llm-sieve`
